names: 50-63% unsalted vegetable fat high in omega 3
50-63% unsalted vegetable fat with plant sterols esters

description: Abondance is a semi-hard, fragrant, raw-milk cheese made in the Haute-Savoie department of France.
The Alaska pollock (Gadus chalcogrammus) is a marine fish species of the cod family Gadidae.
The albacore (Thunnus alalunga), known also as the longfin tuna, is a species of tuna of the order Perciformes.
variation of noodles with a much higher quantity of alkali than usual. The most common examples are Chinese Lamian noodles and Japanese Ramen noodles.

The almond (Prunus amygdalus, syn. Prunus dulcis) is a species of tree native to Iran and surrounding countries, but widely cultivated elsewhere. The almond is also the name of the edible and widely cultivated seed of this tree.
ALOE VERA is a succulent plant species of the genus Aloe.
Amaranthus, collectively known as amaranth is a cosmopolitan genus of annual or short-lived perennial plants.

Placopecten magellanicus, common names Atlantic deep-sea scallop, deep sea scallop, North Atlantic sea scallop, American sea scallop, Atlantic sea scallop, or sea scallop, is a commercially important pectinid bivalve mollusk native to the northwest Atlantic Ocean.

aliases: apple juice made from fresh apples

names: apricot juice from concentrate and puree
aliases: Apricot juice made from cremogenate and concentrate

description: The Arctic char or Arctic charr (Salvelinus alpinus) is a cold-water fish in the family Salmonidae, native to alpine lakes and arctic and subarctic coastal waters. The flesh is fine-flaked and medium firm. The colour is between light pink and deep red, and the taste is like something between trout and salmon.
argan oil is found in argan tree
The arrowtooth flounder (Atheresthes stomas) is a fish in the family Pleuronectidae.

The globe ARTICHOKE (Cynara cardunculus var. scolymus), also known as French artichoke and green artichoke in the USA, is a variety of a species of thistle cultivated as a food. Leaves are often removed one at a time, and the fleshy base eaten, with hollandaise, vinegar, butter, mayonnaise, aioli, lemon juice, or other sauces. The fibrous upper part of each leaf is usually discarded. The heart is eaten when the inedible choke has been peeled away from the base and discarded. The thin leaves covering the choke are also edible.
The heart is eaten when the inedible choke has been peeled away from the base and discarded.
A resinous gum from the stem and roots of Ferula species such as Ferula foetida and Ferula assa-foetida, having a strong, unpleasant smell, with culinary and medical uses
species of flowering plant in the carrot family
Asiago is an Italian cow's milk cheese that can assume different textures, according to its aging, from smooth for the fresh Asiago to a crumbly texture for the aged cheese.
Asparagus, or garden asparagus, folk name sparrow grass, scientific name Asparagus officinalis is a spring vegetable, a flowering perennial plant species in the genus Asparagus;
The Atlantic salmon (Salmo salar) is a species of ray-finned fish in the family Salmonidae.

The Atlantic wolffish (Anarhichas lupus), also known as the seawolf, Atlantic catfish, ocean catfish, devil fish, wolf eel (the common name for its Pacific relative), woof or sea cat, is a marine fish of the wolffish family Anarhichadidae.

The AVOCADO (Persea americana) is a tree classified as a member of the flowering plant family Lauraceae. The fruit of the plant, also called an avocado, is botanically a large berry containing a single large seed.
Baker's yeast is the common name for the strains of yeast commonly used as a leavening agent in baking bread and bakery products
barley (hordeum vulgare), a member of the grass family, is a major cereal grain grown in temperate climates globally
BASMATI RICE is a variety of long, slender-grained aromatic rice which is traditionally from the Indian subcontinent.
Trousseau or Trousseau Noir, also known as Bastardo, is an old variety of red wine grape originating in eastern France.
BÉARNAISE SAUCE is a sauce made of clarified butter emulsified in egg yolks and white wine vinegar and flavored with herbs.
Beaufort is a firm, raw cow's milk cheese associated with the gruyère family.
A BISCUIT is a flour-based baked food product, which is typically hard, flat and unleavened.
The black seabream (Spondyliosoma cantharus) is a species of Sparidae fishes. They live in northern Europe and in the Mediterranean.

names: Blend of EU and non-EU honeys

description: Bleu d'Auvergne is a French AOP blue cheese, made from cow's milk, named for its place of origin in the Auvergne region of south-central France.
Bleu de Bresse is a blue cheese made from whole milk.
Bleu de Gex (also Bleu du Haut-Jura or Bleu de Septmoncel) is a creamy, semi-soft blue cheese made from unpasteurized milk in the Jura region of France.

Blue cheese or bleu cheese is cheese made with cultures of the mold Penicillium, giving it spots or veins of the mold throughout the cheese, which can vary in color through various shades of blue and green.
The blue whiting, Micromesistius poutassou, one of the two species in the genus Micromesistius in the cod family, is common in the northeast Atlantic Ocean from Morocco to Iceland and Spitsbergen
Boiled eggs are eggs, typically from a chicken, cooked with their shells unbroken, usually by immersion in boiling water.
BOLOGNESE SAUCE is a meat-based sauce in Italian cuisine. It is customarily used to dress tagliatelle al ragù and to prepare lasagne alla bolognese.
Borage, also known as a starflower, is an annual herb in the flowering plant family Boraginaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region. Borage is used as either a fresh vegetable or a dried herb.
The borlotti bean is a variety of common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) first bred in Colombia as the cargamanto.
The Brazil nut (Bertholletia excelsa) is a South American tree in the family Lecythidaceae, and it is also the name of the tree's commercially harvested edible seeds.

SACCHAROMYCES CEREVISIAE is a species of yeast. It has been instrumental to winemaking, baking, and brewing since ancient times. S. cerevisiae is used in baking; the carbon dioxide generated by the fermentation is used as a leavening agent in bread and other baked goods. Historically, this use was closely linked to the brewing industry's use of yeast, as bakers took or bought the barm or yeast-filled foam from brewing ale from the brewers (producing the barm cake); today, brewing and baking yeast strains are somewhat different.
Brie is a soft cow's-milk cheese named after Brie, the French region from which it originated. It is pale in color with a slight grayish tinge under a rind of white mould.

aliases: Brie de Meaux cheese from cow's milk
description: Brie de Meaux is an unpasteurized brie, manufactured in the town of Meaux in the Brie region of northern France since the 8th century.

aliases: Brie de Melun cheese from cow's milk
description: Brie de Melun is a brie made with unpasteurized milk.
Brioche is a bread of French origin that is similar to a highly enriched pastry, and whose high egg and butter content give it a rich and tender crumb.
Escarole, or broad-leaved endive (var latifolia), has broad, pale green leaves and is less bitter than the other varieties. Varieties or names include Batavian endive, grumolo, scarola, and scarole.
The burbot (Lota lota) is the only gadiform (cod-like) freshwater fish.

CAKE is a form of sweet food that is usually baked. The most commonly used cake ingredients include flour, sugar, eggs, butter or oil or margarine, a liquid, and leavening agents, such as baking soda or baking powder.

names: Camargue whole long grain black rice

description: CAMELINA sativa is a flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae and is usually known in English as camelina. The crop is being researched due to its exceptionally high level (up to 45%) of omega-3 fatty acids, which is uncommon in vegetable sources.
CAMEMBERT is a moist, soft, creamy, surface-ripened cow's milk cheese from Normandy, France.

Since the 6th century BC, cane sugar producers have crushed the harvested vegetable material from sugarcane in order to collect and filter the juice. They then treat the liquid (often with lime (calcium oxide)) to remove impurities and then neutralize it. Boiling the juice then allows the sediment to settle to the bottom for dredging out, while the scum rises to the surface for skimming off.
Canola oil, or canola for short, originally known as low erucic acid rapeseed, is a vegetable oil derived from a variety of rapeseed that is low in erucic acid, as opposed to colza oil.
Cantal cheese is an uncooked firm cheese produced in the Auvergne region of central France.

names: Cantal cheese half matured from cow's milk

description: Cantaloupe (muskmelon, mushmelon, rockmelon, sweet melon) or spanspek (South Africa) is a variety of the Cucumis melo species in the Cucurbitaceae family.

The capelin or caplin (Mallotus villosus) is a small forage fish of the smelt family found in the North Atlantic, North Pacific, and Arctic oceans. Commercialement, le capelan est utilisé comme produit alimentaire, et dans l'industrie des huiles de poisson. Sa chair a un goût agréable, ressemblant à celle du hareng. Les œufs de capelan ("masago") sont des produits alimentaires coûteux.

Carob pods are mildly sweet on their own, so they are used in powdered, chip or syrup form as an ingredient in cakes and cookies, sometimes as a substitute for chocolate in recipes because of the color, texture, and taste of carob.
Locust bean gum is produced from the endosperm, which accounts for 42–46% of the carob seed

Carotenoids are yellow, orange, and red organic pigments that are produced by plants and algae, as well as several bacteria and fungi. Carotenoids from the diet are stored in the fatty tissues of animals. There is insufficient evidence to state that carotenoids have an antioxidant function in humans or that carotenoid dietary supplements lower the risk of or prevent diseases.

Casein is a family of related phosphoproteins. These proteins are commonly found in mammalian milk, comprising c. 80% of the proteins in cow's milk and between 20% and 45% of the proteins in human milk.
Caster sugar is finer than granulated sugar, but coarser than powdered/icing sugar, with a grain size of about 0.35 mm.
CELERY (Apium graveolens) is a marshland plant in the family Apiaceae that has been cultivated as a vegetable since antiquity.
AGARICUS BISPORUS is an edible basidiomycete mushroom native to grasslands in Europe and North America.
CHEDDAR cheese is a relatively hard, off-white, sometimes sharp-tasting, natural cheese.

names: cherry juice from concentrated cherry juice

description: The chestnuts are a group of eight or nine species of deciduous trees and shrubs in the genus Castanea. The name also refers to the edible nuts they produce. The sweet chestnut produces an edible seed, the chestnut, which has been used in cooking since ancient times.
Chestnuts can be dried and milled into flour, which can then be used to prepare breads, cakes, pies, pancakes, pastas, polenta, or used as thickener for stews, soups, and sauces.
SALVIA HISPANICA, commonly known as CHIA, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae
Chia is grown commercially for its seed, a food rich in omega-3 fatty acids since the seeds yield 25–30% extractable oil, including α-linolenic acid.
Chicken Cutlet is a thin slice of meat from the leg or ribs of chicken.

names: chicken meat including natural chicken juices
aliases: chicken meat and natural chicken juices

description: CHLORELLA is a genus of single-celled green algae belonging to the division Chlorophyta.

names: chocolate flavor filling with pieces of coconut
chocolate-flavored paste with ground cocoa biscuits

aliases: Spicy pork sausage with red pepper no precision
description: CHORIZO or chouriço is a type of pork sausage. In Europe, chorizo is a fermented, cured, smoked sausage, which may be sliced and eaten without cooking, or added as an ingredient to add flavor to other dishes. Elsewhere, some sausages sold as chorizo may not be fermented and cured, and require cooking before eating. Spanish chorizo and Portuguese chouriço get their distinctive smokiness and deep red color from dried, smoked, red peppers.
Citrus unshiu is a semi-seedless and easy-peeling citrus species, also known as miyagawa mandarin, unshu mikan, cold hardy mandarin, satsuma mandarin, satsuma orange, naartjie, and tangerine.

names: cocoa powder with reduced cocoa butter content

description: COCONUT SUGAR is a palm sugar produced from the sap of the flower bud stem of the coconut palm. It is used in savory dishes, but mainly in local desserts and cakes of the Southeast Asian region. Coconut sugar is subtly sweet almost like brown sugar but with a slight hint of caramel. The flavor and sweetness is usually similar to table sugar or brown sugar. There is no scientific evidence that coconut sugar is more nutritious or healthier than any other sweetener.
Type of semi-hard cheese.
Colza oil or colza is a non-drying oil obtained from the seeds of rapeseed (Brassica napus subsp. napus).

The common dentex (Dentex dentex) is a species of fish in the family Sparidae. Dentex is common in the Mediterranean Sea, but also seen in the Black Sea and the Eastern Atlantic Ocean from the British Isles to Mauritania.

The common sole, Dover sole, or black sole (Solea solea) is a species of flatfish in the family Soleidae.It is a largest fish in solea (fish) genus. Chefs prize Dover sole for its mild, buttery, sweet flavour and versatility, and for its ease of filleting.
COMTÉ (or Gruyère de Comté) is a French cheese made from unpasteurized cow's milk in the Franche-Comté region of eastern France.
CONCENTRATE is the form of substance which has had the majority of its base component (in the case of a liquid: the solvent) removed

description: Coriander (Coriandrum sativum), also known as cilantro or Chinese parsley, is an annual herb in the family Apiaceae.
Corn oil (maize oil) is oil extracted from the germ of corn (maize).
type of dry, firm grating cheese from Mexico.
CRABS are decapod crustaceans of the infraorder Brachyura, which typically have a very short projecting "tail".
CRAYFISH, also known as crawfish, crawdads, crawlfish,[1] crawldads,[2] freshwater lobsters, mountain lobsters, mudbugs, or yabbies, are freshwater crustaceans resembling small lobsters
CREAM is a dairy product composed of the higher-butterfat layer skimmed from the top of milk before homogenization.

description: Crottin de Chavignol is a goat cheese produced in the Loire Valley.
A CROUTON is a piece of sautéed or rebaked bread, often cubed and seasoned, that is used to add texture and flavor to salads as an accompaniment to soups and stews, or eaten as a snack food.
CUCUMBER (Cucumis sativus) is a widely cultivated plant in the gourd family, Cucurbitaceae.
CURING SALT is used in meat processing to generate a pinkish shade and to extend shelf life.
CURRY PASTE usually refers to a paste used as a cooking ingredient in the preparation of a curry.
DARK BLACK CHOCOLATE is a form of chocolate containing cocoa solids, cocoa butter and sugar, without the milk found in milk chocolate.
Dark syrup is similar to a mix of golden syrup and black treacle, being thinner and with a less pronounced flavour than that of black treacle.

DATE, the fruit of the date palm (Phoenix dactylifera). Dry or soft dates are eaten out-of-hand, or may be pitted and stuffed with fillings such as almonds, walnuts, pecans, candied orange and lemon peel, tahini, marzipan or cream cheese.

description: A kind of mustard made with verjuice (or, more recently, white wine) instead of vinegar.
dill (anethum graveolens) is an annual herb in the celery family apiaceae. dill is widely grown in eurasia where its leaves and seeds are used as a herb or spice for flavouring food.

Gloucester is a traditional, semi-hard cheese which has been made in Gloucestershire, England, since the 16th century, at one time made only with the milk of the once nearly extinct Gloucester cattle.

Palmaria palmata, also called dulse, dillisk or dilsk (from Irish/Scottish Gaelic duileasc/duileasg), red dulse, sea lettuce flakes, or creathnach, is a red alga (Rhodophyta) previously referred to as Rhodymenia palmata.
EDAM is a semi-hard cheese that originated in the Netherlands, and is named after the town of Edam in the province of North Holland.

description: EGGS are laid by female animals of many different species, including birds, reptiles, amphibians, mammals, and fish. This ingredient comprises mainly chicken eggs.
EGG WHITE is the clear liquid (also called the albumen or the glair/glaire) contained within an egg.

description: EMMENTAL (Emmentaler or Emmenthal) is a yellow, medium-hard Swiss cheese that originated in the area around Emmental, Canton Bern.

description: The Espelette pepper is a variety of Capsicum annuum that is cultivated in the French commune of Espelette, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, traditionally the northern territory of the Basque people. On 1 June 2000, it was classified as an AOC product and was confirmed as an APO product on 22 August 2002.
The European anchovy (Engraulis encrasicolus) is a forage fish somewhat related to the herring.

The European perch (Perca fluviatilis), also known as the common perch, redfin perch, big-scaled redfin, English perch, Eurasian perch, Eurasian river perch, Hatch or in Anglophone parts of Europe, simply the perch, is a predatory species of the freshwater perch native to Europe and northern Asia.
The European plaice (Pleuronectes platessa) is a commercially important flatfish
The European bass (Dicentrarchus labrax) is a primarily ocean-going fish native to the waters off Europe's western and southern and Africa's northern coasts.

description: An extract is a substance made by extracting a part of a raw material, often by using a solvent such as ethanol or water. Extracts may be sold as tinctures, absolutes or in powder form.
Faisselle is a non-protected French cheese made of raw milk from cows, goats, or sheep.

description: FERMENT is an agent, such as an enzyme, bacterium, or fungus, that brings about fermentation.

description: Fish are gill-bearing aquatic craniate animals that lack limbs with digits.
FISH OIL is oil derived from the tissues of oily fish
The flat peach (Prunus persica var. platycarpa), also known as the doughnut peach or Saturn peach, is a variety of peach with pale yellow fruit that is oblate in shape.
Flax, also known as common flax or linseed, is a flowering plant, Linum usitatissimum, in the family Linaceae. Flax seeds occur in two basic varieties/colors: brown or yellow (golden linseeds).
FLEUR DE SEL - is a salt that forms as a thin, delicate crust on the surface of seawater as it evaporates.
Fontina is a cow's milk cheese, produced worldwide.
Fontina AOP is an cow's milk cheese, produced in the Aosta Valley.
Fourme d'Ambert is a semi-hard French AOC blue cheese, made from raw cow's milk from the Auvergne region of France.
Fourme de Montbrison is a cow's-milk cheese made in the regions of Rhône-Alpes and Auvergne in southern France.
Bream are species of freshwater and marine fish belonging to a variety of genera.
Frillice iceberg lettuce is a cross of curly endive and iceberg lettuce, with the taste and texture of iceberg and the frilly top of the curly endive.

names: fruit juices and purees from concentrate

description: Fucus is a genus of brown algae found in the intertidal zones of rocky seashores almost throughout the world.
GELATIN or GELATINE is a translucent, colorless, brittle (when dry), flavorless food ingredient that is derived from collagen obtained from various animal body parts.
GHERKIN can refer to either a pickled small cucumber (esp. in Ireland and the UK) or an unpickled small cucumber
GINGER (Zingiber officinale) is a flowering plant whose rhizome, ginger root or simply ginger, is widely used as a spice or a folk medicine
GINGERBREAD refers to a broad category of baked goods, typically flavored with ginger, cloves, nutmeg or cinnamon and sweetened with honey, sugar or molasses.

Salicornia is a genus of succulent, halophyte (salt tolerant) flowering plants in the family Amaranthaceae that grow in salt marshes, on beaches, and among mangroves. The main European species is often eaten and the main North American species is occasionally sold in grocery stores or appears on restaurant menus, usually as 'sea beans' or samphire greens or sea asparagus.

GLYCEROL MONOSTEARATE, commonly known as GMS, is a monoglyceride commonly used as an emulsifier in foods. GMS is a food additive used as a thickening, emulsifying, anticaking, and preservative agent; an emulsifying agent for oils, waxes, and solvents; a protective coating for hygroscopic powders; a solidifier and control release agent in pharmaceuticals; and a resin lubricant.
Goat milk is the milk of domestic goats.
CANTHARELLUS CIBARIUS is a species of golden chanterelle mushroom in the genus Cantharellus.
GORGONZOLA is a veined Italian blue cheese, made from unskimmed cow's milk.
GOUDA is a mild, yellow cheese made from cow's milk.

aliases: Grana Padano cheese from cow's milk
description: GRANA PADANO is a hard, slow-ripened, semi-fat, cow's-milk cheese from Italy.
Granulated, familiar as table sugar, with a grain size about 0.5-0.6 mm across.
GRAPE SEED OIL (also called grapeseed oil or grape oil) is pressed from the seeds of grapes, and is thus an abundant by-product of winemaking.

names: grapefruit or pomelo juice from concentrate

description: The Greenland halibut or Greenland turbot (Reinhardtius hippoglossoides) belongs to the family Pleuronectidae.
Groupers are fish of any of a number of genera in the subfamily Epinephelinae of the family Serranidae, in the order Perciformes.
Gruyere is a hard cheese mad from cow milk. It originates either from Switzerland or France.
GRUYÈRE is a hard yellow cheese that originated in the cantons of Fribourg, Vaud, Neuchâtel, Jura, and Bern in Switzerland.

aliases: Gruyere cheese France Protected Geographical Indication from cow's milk

description: HARISSA is a Tunisian hot chili pepper paste, the main ingredients of which are roasted red peppers, Baklouti pepper, serrano peppers, and other hot chili peppers, spices and herbs such as garlic paste, coriander seed, saffron, rose, or caraway, as well as some vegetable or olive oil to carry the oil-soluble flavors.
semisoft Danish cow's milk cheese.

description: A type of small, oily fish of the genus Clupea, often used as food.
HONEY is a sweet, viscous food substance produced by bees and some related insects.
Honjōzō (本醸造, 'genuine fermented'): Contains 100% genuine fermented product
Horse mackerel is a vague vernacular term for a range of species of fish throughout the English-speaking world.
Hulled barley is a whole grain barley with only the inedible outer shell removed.

ICE CREAM is a sweetened frozen food typically eaten as a snack or dessert. It may be made from dairy milk or cream, or soy, cashew, coconut or almondmilk, and is flavored with a sweetener, either sugar or an alternative, and any spice, such as cocoa or vanilla. Colourings are usually added, in addition to stabilizers.
ILLIPE butter is a vegetable fat from the nut of the Shorea stenoptera tree.
Imazalil is a fungicide widely used in agriculture, particularly in the growing of citrus fruits.
IMLERIA is a genus of mushrooms from the Boletaceae family
Uroteuthis duvaucelii, also known as the Indian Ocean Squid or Indian Squid, is an Indo-West Pacific species of squid with a wide range throughout the Indian Ocean
INSTANT COFFEE is a beverage derived from brewed coffee beans that enables people to quickly prepare hot coffee by adding hot water or milk to the powder or crystals and stirring
IODISED SALT - is salt mixed with a minute amount of various salts of the element iodine.
type of cured surimi, a processed seafood product common in Japanese cuisine

simmered, smoked and fermented skipjack tuna
A kirschwasser or kirsch is a clear, colorless fruit brandy traditionally made from double distillation of morello cherries, a dark-colored cultivar of the sour cherry.

KOMBU (from Japanese: 昆布, translit. konbu) is edible kelp from mostly the family Laminariaceae and is widely eaten in East Asia. There are about eighteen edible species in Laminariaceae and most of them, but not all, are called kombu.
Konjac is a common name of the East and Southeast Asian plant Amorphophallus konjac (syn. A. rivieri), which has an edible corm (bulbo-tuber).
Kosher salt or kitchen salt[1] is edible salt with a larger grain size than typical table salt and without common additives such as iodine.
Langres is a French cheese from the plateau of Langres in the region of Champagne-Ardenne.
LECCINUM is a genus of mushrooms from the Boletaceae family

A LEGUME is a plant in the family Fabaceae (or Leguminosae), or the fruit or seed of such a plant (also called a pulse). Legumes are grown agriculturally, primarily for human consumption. Grain legumes include beans, lentils, lupins, peas, and peanuts.
Light syrup is similar to Golden Syrup, but is made slightly differently and is a bit thinner.
BEURRE DEMI-SEL is a butter with a salt percentage between 0,5 % and 3 %
Molva est un genre de poissons appelés lingues, élingues ou juliennes.
LIQUORICE or licorice is the root of Glycyrrhiza glabra from which a sweet flavour can be extracted.
Livarot is a French cheese of the Normandy region, originating in the commune of Livarot.
LOBSTERS comprise a family (Nephropidae, sometimes also Homaridae) of large marine crustaceans.
LYCHEE is the sole member of the genus Litchi in the soapberry family, Sapindaceae.
MAASDAM cheese is a Swiss-style Dutch cheese.

description: Mackerel is a common name applied to a number of different species of pelagic fish, mostly from the family Scombridae.

names: malted wholemeal rye flakes and flour

description: MALTOSE, also known as maltobiose or malt sugar, is a disaccharide formed from two units of glucose joined. Maltose is a component of malt, a substance which is obtained in the process of allowing grain to soften in water and germinate. It is also present in highly variable quantities in partially hydrolysed starch products like maltodextrin, corn syrup and acid-thinned starch. In humans, maltose is broken down by various maltase enzymes, providing two glucose molecules which can be further processed: either broken down to provide energy, or stored as glycogen. The lack of the sucrase-isomaltase enzyme in humans causes sucrose intolerance.
MARGARINE is a spread used for flavoring, baking, and cooking. Margarine is made mainly of refined vegetable oil and water.
Maroilles is a cow's-milk cheese made in the regions of Picardy and Nord-Pas-de-Calais in northern France.
MARZIPAN is a confection consisting primarily of sugar or honey and almond meal (ground almonds), sometimes augmented with almond oil or extract.

Mate is a traditional South American caffeine-rich infused drink. It is prepared by steeping dried leaves of yerba mate in hot water and is served with a metal straw from a shared hollow calabash gourd

MAYONNAISE, informally mayo, is a thick cold sauce or dressing usually used in sandwiches and composed salads or on chips. It is a stable emulsion of oil, egg yolk, and acid, either vinegar or lemon juice.
Meringue is a type of dessert or candy, traditionally made from whipped egg whites and sugar.
MILK CHOCOLATE is solid chocolate made with milk added in the form of powdered milk, liquid milk, or condensed milk.
LACTARIUS is a genus of mushroom-producing, ectomycorrhizal fungi, containing several edible species.
Millets are a group of highly variable small-seeded grasses, widely grown around the world as cereal crops or grains for fodder and human food
Mimolette is a cheese traditionally produced around the city of Lille, France.
MISO is a traditional Japanese seasoning produced by fermenting soybeans with salt and kōji (the fungus Aspergillus oryzae) and sometimes rice, barley, seaweed or other ingredients.

mixture of palm oil and palm superolein

Molasses (American English) or black treacle (British English) is a viscous product resulting from refining sugarcane or sugar beets into sugar.

description: MONTEREY JACK, sometimes shortened to Jack, is an American white, semi-hard cheese made using cow's milk. It is noted for its mild flavor and slight sweetness.
Morbier is an AOP semi-soft cows' milk cheese of France.
Mozzarella is a traditionally southern Italian cheese made from Italian buffalo's milk by the pasta filata method.
American cheese.
Munster, Munster-géromé, or Minschterkaas, is a strong-smelling soft cheese with a subtle taste, made mainly from milk first produced in the Vosges.
Powder or paste made from seeds of the mustard plant, and used as a condiment or a spice.

The Nile perch (Lates niloticus), also known as the African snook, Goliath perch, Giant lates or the Victoria perch, is a species of freshwater fish in family Latidae of order Perciformes. It is widespread throughout much of the Afrotropical realm.

PAPRIKA OLEORESIN (also known as PAPRIKA EXTRACT and oleoresin paprika) is an oil-soluble extract from the fruits of Capsicum annuum or Capsicum frutescens, and is primarily used as a colouring and/or flavouring in food products.

description: PARMIGIANO-REGGIANO is an Italian hard, granular cheese.
Doryteuthis gahi, also known as the Patagonian squid and locally as calamar, is a small-sized squid belonging to the family Loliginidae.

The peach (Prunus persica) is a deciduous tree native to the region of Northwest China. It bears edible juicy fruits with various characteristics, most called peaches and others (the glossy-skinned varieties), nectarines.
PEANUT OIL, also known as groundnut oil or arachis oil, is a mild-tasting vegetable oil derived from peanuts.
Pearl barley is barley that has been processed to remove its fibrous outer hull and polished to remove some or all of the bran layer.
Argopecten purpuratus, common names the Peruvian scallop, is an edible species of saltwater clam, a scallop.
PESTO is a sauce originating in Genoa, Italy. It traditionally consists of crushed garlic, European pine nuts, coarse salt, basil leaves, hard cheese, all blended with olive oil.

The "PETIT BEURRE", or "Véritable Petit Beurre", also known under the initials "VPB", is a kind of shortbread from Nantes, that is best known in France. It is the Petit Beurre of the LU society, which has become a success worldwide.
Picodon is a goats-milk cheese made in the region around the Rhône in southern France.

A PINK PEPPERCORN is a dried berry of the shrub Schinus molle, commonly known as the Peruvian peppertree. Although a peppercorn is the dried fruit of a plant from the genus Piper, pink peppercorns came to be called such because they resemble peppercorns, and because they, too, have a peppery flavour. As they are members of the cashew family, they may cause allergic reactions including anaphylaxis for persons with a tree nut allergy.

description: Pont-l'Évêque is an uncooked, unpressed cow's-milk cheese, originally manufactured in the area around the commune of Pont-l'Évêque, in the Calvados département of Normandy.
The seed of the poppy.
Pork belly is a boneless and fatty cut of meat from the belly of a pig.
Pork tenderloin, also called pork fillet, pork steak[1] or Gentleman's Cut, is a long thin cut of pork.
Pork loin is a cut of meat from a pig, created from the tissue along the dorsal side of the rib cage.
Pošip is an autochthonous white wine grape that is primarily grown in the Dalmatian region of Croatia on the island of Korčula, although small amounts are also being grown on the Pelješac Peninsula.
Pot barley is barley that has been processed to remove its fibrous outer hull but has not been polished.
Poulsard (also Ploussard) is a red French wine grape variety from the Jura wine region.
Poultry are domesticated birds kept by humans for their eggs, their meat or their feathers.
Trisopterus luscus (bib, pout whiting, pout or most commonly pouting) is a seafish belonging to the cod family (Gadidae).

description: PROTEINS are large biomolecules, or macromolecules, consisting of one or more long chains of amino acid residues.

Psyllium, or ispaghula, is the common name used for several members of the plant genus Plantago whose seeds are used commercially for the production of mucilage. It can be used for the fibres or as infusion.
A quenelle is a mixture of creamed fish or meat, sometimes combined with breadcrumbs, with a light egg binding, formed into an egg-like shape, and then cooked.
The QUINCE (Cydonia oblonga) is the sole member of the genus Cydonia in the family Rosaceae (which also contains apples and pears, among other fruits).
QUORN is a meat substitute product originating in the UK and sold primarily in Europe, but also available in 19 countries.
RACLETTE is a semi-hard cow's milk cheese.
The rainbow trout (Oncorhynchus mykiss) is a trout and species of salmonid native to cold-water tributaries of the Pacific Ocean in Asia and North America.

Rapeseed (Brassica napus), also known as rape, or oilseed rape, is a bright-yellow flowering member of the family Brassicaceae, cultivated mainly for its oil-rich seed, which naturally contains appreciable amounts of erucic acid. Rapeseed is the third-largest source of vegetable oil and the second-largest source of protein meal in the world.

rye (secale cereale) is a grass grown extensively as a grain, a cover crop and a forage crop.

description: Le Sainte-maure-de-touraine est un fromage de chèvre1 de la région Centre-Val de Loire, en France. Cette appellation d'origine doit son existence à la commune homonyme Sainte-Maure-de-Touraine. L'appellation Sainte-maure-de-touraine est protégée par un label régional depuis 1978, puis depuis le 29 juin 1990, par une appellation d'origine protégée.
The saithe (Pollachius virens) is a species of marine fish in the Pollachius genus.
Salami is a cured sausage consisting of fermented and air-dried meat, typically pork.
ROCKFISH is a common term for several species of fish, referring to their tendency to hide among rocks.
Salers is a French semi-hard com milk cheese originating from Salers, located in the volcanic region in the Cantal mountains of Massif Central, central France.
Salmon is the common name for several species of ray-finned fish in the family Salmonidae.
Mexican table sauces, especially the chunky tomato-and-chili-based pico de gallo.
SALT - is a mineral composed primarily of sodium chloride (NaCl), a chemical compound belonging to the larger class of salts "sodium chloride" is defined in minerals.txt

Sambal is an Indonesian chili sauce or paste typically made from a mixture of a variety of chili peppers with secondary ingredients such as shrimp paste, garlic, ginger, shallot, scallion, palm sugar, and lime juice.
Raw chili paste (bright red, thin and sharp tasting). Can be used as the base for making other sambals or as an ingredient for other cuisines.
large species of bamboo grass
Scallop is a common name that is primarily applied to any one of numerous species of saltwater clams or marine bivalve mollusks in the taxonomic family Pectinidae, the scallops.
The sea lettuces comprise the genus Ulva, a group of edible green algae that is widely distributed along the coasts of the world's oceans.
SEA SALT - is salt that is produced by the evaporation of seawater
The Sparidae are a family of fish in the order Perciformes, commonly called sea breams and porgies.

description: Sémillon is a golden-skinned grape used to make dry and sweet white wines, mostly in France and Australia.
Semolina is the coarse, purified wheat middlings of durum wheat mainly used in making pasta and couscous.
SESAME OIL is an edible vegetable oil derived from sesame seeds.

SHEA BUTTER is a fat extracted from the nut of the African shea tree (Vitellaria paradoxa). Shea butter is edible and is used in food preparation in some African countries. Occasionally, shea butter is mixed with other oils as a substitute for cocoa butter, although the taste is noticeably different.
Sheep's milk (or ewes' milk) is the milk of domestic sheep.
Shellfish is a food source and fisheries term for exoskeleton-bearing aquatic invertebrates used as food, including various species of molluscs, crustaceans, and echinoderms.
Sichuan pepper is a spice made from the dried pericarp (outer shell of the fruit) of a plant of the genus Zanthoxylum in the family Rutaceae. (Wikipedia)
The skipjack tuna (Katsuwonus pelamis) is a medium-sized perciform fish in the tuna family, Scombridae.
Sodium iodide (chemical formula NaI) is an ionic compound formed from the chemical reaction of sodium metal and iodine.

SOY MILK or soymilk is a plant-based drink produced by soaking and grinding soybeans, boiling the mixture, and filtering out remaining particulates.

The striped bass (Morone saxatilis), also called the Atlantic striped bass, striper, linesider, rock or rockfish, is an anadromous perciform fish of the family Moronidae found primarily along the Atlantic coast of North America.
Sun-dried tomatoes are ripe tomatoes that lose most of their water content after spending a majority of their drying time in the sun.
SUNFLOWER OIL is the non-volatile oil pressed from the seeds of sunflower (Helianthus annuus).

Sylvaner or Silvaner is a variety of white wine grape grown primarily in Alsace and Germany, where its official name is Grüner Silvaner.
Syrah, also known as Shiraz, is a dark-skinned grape variety grown throughout the world and used primarily to produce red wine.
Tahini is a condiment made from toasted ground hulled sesame.
TAPIOCA is a starch extracted from cassava root (tapioca seems to be the same as tapioca starch, but not used as such on ingredient lists)
TEQUILA is a regional distilled beverage and type of alcoholic drink made from the blue agave plant, primarily in the area surrounding the city of Tequila
Teran grape is a variety of red grape from Istria, Croatia.

JASMINE RICE is a long-grain variety of fragrant rice (also known as aromatic rice). Its fragrance, reminiscent of jasmine and popcorn, results from the rice plant's natural production of aromatic compounds
HIMANTHALIA ELONGATA is a brown alga (class Phaeophyta or Phaeophyceae) in the order Fucales, also known by the common names thongweed, sea thong and sea spaghetti.
“Oil made from toasted seeds is used for its distinctive nutty aroma and taste, although it may be unsuitable for frying, which makes it taste burnt and bitter.” (Wikipedia)
TOMME, occasionally spelled Tome, is a class of cheese produced mainly in the French Alps and in Switzerland.

VINAIGRE is a liquid consisting of about 5–20% acetic acid (CH3COOH), water (H2O), and trace chemicals that may include flavorings.
A walnut is the nut of any tree of the genus Juglans. After full ripening for its edible seed when the shell has been discarded, it is used as a garnish or a snack.
WALNUT OIL is oil extracted from walnuts, Juglans regia

Allium fistulosum, the Welsh onion, also commonly called bunching onion, long green onion, Japanese bunching onion, and spring onion, is a species of perennial plant. A. fistulosum is an ingredient in Asian cuisine, especially in East and Southeast Asia. In the West, A. fistulosum is primarily used as a scallion or salad onion.
WHEY PROTEIN is a mixture of globular proteins isolated from whey, the liquid material created as a by-product of cheese production.
WHITE CHOCOLATE is made of sugar, milk, and cocoa butter, without the cocoa solids. It is pale ivory colour, and lacks many of the compounds found in milk and dark chocolates.
WHITE WINE is a wine whose colour can be straw-yellow, yellow-green, or yellow-gold.

Whiteleg shrimp (Litopenaeus vannamei, formerly Penaeus vannamei), also known as Pacific white shrimp or king prawn, is a variety of prawn of the eastern Pacific Ocean commonly caught or farmed for food.

The yellowfin tuna (Thunnus albacares) is a species of tuna found in pelagic waters of tropical and subtropical oceans worldwide.
